body.title: cc_queue.py next --max-difficulty=N filters by target_difficulty
body.result: next_ticket_id_for_worker gains max_difficulty param; cmd_next parses --max-difficulty=N. 9 tests. Commits ffbe2c6 + 6b2f41dd.
body.description:
Consumers need to declare capability so they only receive tickets they can execute. Add `--max-difficulty=N` flag to `cc_queue.py next`. When set, only return the next ticket where `target_difficulty <= N` (tickets with no difficulty field default to 1). Without the flag, `next` returns any ticket (no filter). Note: T-queue-gate must also be complete since `next` command is added there; this ticket extends it.

**Affected files:** ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py
**Design rules:** backward-compat (no flag = no filter); unset-difficulty treated as 1
**Scope boundary:** --max-difficulty filtering only. No schema changes (T-ticket-difficulty-schema). No daemon/config changes (T-igor-difficulty-cap). Depends on T-queue-gate for the `next` command to exist.
**Test plan:** `next --max-difficulty=1` skips a manually-inserted difficulty=2 ticket; `next --max-difficulty=5` returns it; `next` with no flag still returns it (backward-compat); a ticket with no difficulty field is treated as difficulty=1 and returned by `--max-difficulty=1`.
